industries.name: Targeted Alpha Therapy (Emerging)
industries.share: ~100% of astatine use — entirely medical research
uses: Astatine-211 (²¹¹At, t½ = 7.2 hours, alpha emitter) is the most promising astatine isotope for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) in cancer treatment — alpha particles travel only 50–80 μm in tissue, depositing lethal doses precisely in tumor cells while sparing surrounding healthy tissue

²¹¹At behaves chemically similarly to iodine — it can be incorporated into organic molecules using radioiodination chemistry, enabling labeling of antibodies, peptides, and small molecules that target tumor antigens

Clinical trials: ²¹¹At-labeled compounds are in Phase I/II trials for brain tumors (glioblastoma), ovarian cancer, melanoma, and leukemia. The astatine-labeled anti-CD33 antibody BC8 is in trials for acute myeloid leukemia

Production bottleneck: ²¹¹At is produced exclusively by cyclotron bombardment of bismuth-209 with alpha particles (²⁰⁹Bi + α → ²¹¹At + 2n) — only ~20 cyclotrons worldwide can produce it, and the 7.2-hour half-life limits distribution radius to ~500 km from production site
Astatine is the rarest naturally occurring element — less than 30g exists in Earth's crust at any given time. All practical astatine is cyclotron-produced

companies.name: RadioMedix / Orano Med
companies.use: Developing ²¹¹At-labeled radiopharmaceuticals for targeted alpha therapy. Orano Med is developing ²¹¹At production infrastructure and clinical-stage ²¹¹At radiopharmaceuticals for oncology applications across European and US clinical trials.

companies.name: Duke University Medical Center
companies.use: One of the few US centers with cyclotron capability and expertise to produce ²¹¹At. Conducting clinical trials of astatine-labeled compounds for brain tumor treatment. A leading center for astatine radiopharmaceutical development.
